design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: Proximal bridge quotient identification without completeness. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are Miao-Geng-Tchetgen Tchetgen proximal causal inference, Tchetgen-Tchetgen/Cui/Shi/Miao proximal AIPW estimators, and existing banked eid_proximal_phase_v1 completeness-phase attempts. Why non-trivial? The target is not another singular-value completeness threshold, support condition, or bridge-existence restatement: require a finite negative-control matrix witness where the outcome bridge equation has multiple solutions but the ATE functional is nevertheless point-identified because the target contrast annihilates the bridge nullspace. The theorem should give an iff quotient certificate: bridge nonuniqueness is harmless exactly when every null bridge has zero target contrast, with a hand-derived 3x3 witness separating bridge uniqueness from target uniqueness. Why promising? The nonroutine object is the quotient bridge class and nullspace-annihilator certificate, not LP duality or bounded sensitivity. If the delta reduces to ordinary linear-system solvability, standard completeness, support coverage, or definition-unfold bridge equivalence, pivot or accept field-tier.

banked_reason: Stage -1.1 rejected the topic as already solved: Zhang, Li, Miao, and Tchetgen Tchetgen already give the bridge-nullspace orthocomplement criterion for identifying a linear functional of a nonunique outcome bridge.
